..summary: object_or_default is a generic helper that restates a ternary None check without added policy

evidence: backend/helpers.py line 121: 'def object_or_default(obj: Any | None, _default: Any) -> Any: if obj is not None: return obj else: return _default'
Used 9 times in routers/openai/assistants.py lines 141-159 for name, description, instructions, model, tools, tool_resources, temperature, top_p, metadata, response_format
detail.suggestion: Inline the expression: use 'request.name or old_assistant.name' where falsy-vs-None distinction does not matter, or 'request.name if request.name is not None else old_assistant.name' where it does. Then remove object_or_default and its tests.

..summary: Multi-line docstrings on trivial one-line getter functions in test utilities

evidence: get_cowabunga_model() has a 6-line docstring for 'return os.getenv("COWABUNGA_MODEL", "vllm")'
get_openai_key() has a 9-line docstring for a 3-line env-var lookup
get_openai_model(), get_cowabunga_api_key(), get_cowabunga_api_url(), get_cowabunga_api_url_base(), openai_client(), cowabunga_client() all repeat the same pattern
detail.suggestion: Replace verbose Google-style docstrings on trivial getters with single-line docstrings or omit them entirely; the types and behavior are self-evident.

..summary: Meaningless 'except Exception as e: raise e' blocks in CRUD base methods

evidence: create() wraps result parsing in try/except that catches Exception and immediately re-raises it without modification
list() and update() repeat the same pattern
The only genuine handler is the IndexError branch in get(); the bare 'except Exception as e: raise e' adds no value and obscures the real control flow
detail.suggestion: Remove the no-op except/raise wrappers; keep only the IndexError guard in get().

..summary: Unnecessary try/except around a file-open that already raises FileNotFoundError

evidence: The function wraps 'with open(data_path, "r"):' in try/except FileNotFoundError just to re-raise FileNotFoundError with a slightly different message
A restating comment 'Check if the file exists' sits directly above the with-open block
detail.suggestion: Remove the try/except wrapper and the restating comment; let the built-in FileNotFoundError propagate naturally, or use data_path.exists() for an explicit guard.

..summary: Restating comments echo the code without adding insight

evidence: '# Create .model directory if it doesn't exist' precedes os.makedirs('.model', exist_ok=True)
'# Check if the model is already downloaded' precedes os.path.exists(OUTPUT_FILE)
'# Download the model using huggingface_hub' precedes hf_hub_download(...)
'# Rename to expected output file if different' precedes os.rename(...)
'# Verify checksum if provided' precedes a hashlib check
detail.suggestion: Delete the restating comments; the code is self-explanatory. Keep only comments that explain WHY (e.g., why HF_HUB_ENABLE_HF_TRANSFER is set).

..summary: Identical copy-pasted try/except → HTTPException boilerplate across OpenAI router files

evidence: assistants.py create_assistant: 'except Exception as exc: raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail="Unable to parse assistant request") from exc'
threads.py modify_thread: identical shape with 'Unable to parse thread request'
runs.py modify_run: identical shape with 'Unable to parse run request'
messages.py create_message: identical shape with 'Unable to parse message request'
files.py upload_file: identical shape with 'Failed to parse file'
A second identical pattern for 500-level errors appears in the same files
detail.suggestion: Extract a small helper in the routers package (e.g., raise_parse_error, raise_db_error) or use a FastAPI exception handler to avoid repeating the same 4-line block in every router.

..summary: CRUDBase.delete catches all exceptions and silently returns False

evidence: CRUDBase.delete contains 'except Exception: return False' which hides database connection errors, permission errors, and syntax errors from callers
detail.suggestion: Let specific exceptions propagate or wrap them in a custom CRUD exception that preserves the original cause. Only return False when the delete genuinely matched zero rows.

..summary: CRUD subclasses use inconsistent filter types: some typed models, some raw dicts

evidence: CRUDAssistant, CRUDVectorStore, and CRUDFileObject override get/list/delete to accept typed filter models (FilterAssistant, FilterVectorStore, FilterFileObject)
CRUDThread, CRUDRun, and CRUDMessage inherit base methods directly and pass raw dict filters like {'id': thread_id}
CRUDAPIKey overrides get/list but still accepts dict | None, not a typed filter model
detail.suggestion: Standardize all CRUD subclasses to use typed filter models that convert to dict internally, or remove typed filter subclasses and consistently use dicts across the layer. Pick one pattern and apply it uniformly.

..summary: Database client backends expose unequal query surfaces and different factory parameter names

evidence: TursoQueryBuilder adds neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, order, limit, range beyond the QueryBuilder ABC
SupabaseQueryBuilder only wraps the Supabase query object and does not expose the extra filter methods
TursoClient.create(base_url, auth_token) vs SupabaseClient.create(supabase_url, supabase_key) — parameter names differ even though semantic roles are identical
Callers depending on backend-specific query capabilities will fail silently when the factory returns the other backend
detail.suggestion: Expand the QueryBuilder ABC to include the common filter methods, implement them for both backends, and align factory parameter names (e.g., url and key for both). Alternatively, document which query operations are backend-specific.

..summary: Public functions return union types based on runtime flags rather than separate endpoints

evidence: Composer.generate_response returns Run | StreamingResponse based on request.stream flag
create_run in runs.py returns Run | StreamingResponse based on runtime branching
chat_complete returns either a ChatCompletionResponse or a StreamingResponse based on req.stream
complete in completions.py similarly branches on req.stream with no return type annotation
detail.suggestion: Either split streaming and non-streaming into separate functions with distinct return types, or document the union return type explicitly and ensure callers handle both cases. Adding return type annotations to chat_complete and complete would also help.

narrative.headline: ⚠ 13 security issues — review before other cleanup. 410 issues open. Test health (0.0%) needs attention — run `desloppify next` to start. (117 review work items — run `desloppify show review --status open`)

actions.description: 70 orphaned issues — delete dead files or relocate with `desloppify move`
1 flat_dirs issues — create subdirectories and use `desloppify move`
21 facade issues — flatten re-export facades or consolidate barrel files
7 structural work items — review large files — split only when responsibilities are clearly separable
57 test_coverage work items — review test coverage gaps — focus on critical modules with many importers first; defer test writing until code quality issues are resolved
15 signature work items — consolidate inconsistent function signatures
3 responsibility_cohesion work items — split modules with too many responsibilities — extract focused sub-modules
7 boilerplate_duplication work items — extract shared boilerplate into reusable helpers or base classes
117 review work items need investigation — run `desloppify show review --status open` to see them
89 smells issues — inspect with `desloppify next` and fix manually
1 global_mutable_config work items — refactor module-level mutable state — use explicit init functions or dependency injection
3 stale_exclude work items — remove stale exclusion or verify it's still needed
13 security work items — review and fix security issues — prioritize by severity
6 assessment requests need review — run holistic review to refresh subjective scores
2.1 pts of wontfix debt — review stale decisions
